description: You are a middle school teacher at an OLPC-equipped school preparing a Roman civilization unit. Using the Sugar Pippy activity, write a Python program that converts decimal numbers 1 through 50 to Roman numerals using correct subtractive notation (e.g., 4 to IV, 49 to XLIX). The program must generate two reference files: a plain text file at /home/ga/Documents/roman_numerals.txt with conversions in the format 'N = ROMAN' (one per line), and an HTML file at /home/ga/Documents/roman_numerals.html containing an HTML table of the same conversions.
